You can't let the truth get in front of this sale. It has to be behind this sale. I have one sleazy thing to tell you. Then we're going to let these two guys in. Yeah, probably. If you want to sort of raid the direct selling industry, I would take Craftmatic, I would take the two bathtub guys, I would take the fire alarm companies, I'd take the VAC guys, probably two or three others now. The scooter guys, very good. And I would go to their home city and you run a big help wanted ad for vice presidents of sales and marketing, describing their VP almost to a T. And you camp out there and do interviews. And by the way, a lot of them are in the same place because they were birthed one birth the other by competitors. So like Minneapolis, there's like three of them. There's like three of them. The sleepmatic bed guys are there in Minneapolis and they spun off from somebody else. So if you make the ad attractive enough, their VP's sales are all coming over for the interview. That's what's going to happen. First of all, you can learn a bunch of shit because when people are trying to get a job, they'll tell you all sorts of crap about the company where they work. Secondly, you might actually find a guy you want and he has the ability to swipe the whole sales force. So that's one way. The other way is most of them, their master dealer, they're still actually in the yellow pages. They're still using yellow pages. If you go look up firearms in the yellow pages, the dealer's there. If you go look up water purer, the dealer's there. So you can pick off the master guys and go in at that level. But this is almost like conversion franchising in a sense. I mean, the fastest way to get coverage is to get the coverage that's already there rather than creating the coverage from scratch. All right, you two in and then we're going to be done for the day. And you've been waiting the longest. I had something like this come up in one of my mastermind groups who was a competitor to the scooter store. And his issue was that he was trying to get to the person that had the issue that needed the scooter. He also sold personal lifts, chair lifts, stair lifts, et cetera. And this sounds like almost identical issue. And after going through this and trying to compete and figure out what the nut was to crack, it turns out in this person's case, and I think it is for you too, it's the kids that you need to persuade. The miracle here, the people that start to lose their kids. It is there. It's not so much with him. But it's because this consumer will respond directly. Whereas to some of the hearing aid, it's often the adult child. To some of the scooters, it's often the adult child. However, it is a path. I don't think it's an essential path as it is in the other. But it is a path. So you're right in that at the consumer level, it is another way to market this, which is to the adult child of the person who is suffering from arthritis. True, a person who is working. Yes. Right. Same kind of thing. And it was just another path, as you say. And the way we cracked it was through putting articles in the local papers, becoming the expert. And what happened was, by default, no one else was writing any articles. I don't deal with elder care or these types of issues. So he started picking up other lines of business. Again, in the lead generation area, more than what he wanted to specialize in. And he's just slowly getting out of being the direct supplier. And it has become the person to go to find out about these things. And that's becoming the lead generation business. So I think you want to aim there. That would be my suggestion as another add-on. Just a quick suggestion. I'm out of prizes, by the way. I'm out of prizes. For the areas that you don't have your sales coverage for yet, you might want to try a hybrid of a puppy dog and a rental. The reason I thought of it is because there was a piece of exercise equipment that I was interested in. It's like $14,000. And so they sent you the CD and then you rent it. And then they sent you off the machine, which I have in my house. And probably at the end of the month, I'm probably not going to want to send it back. I know somebody is in pain. They have this machine or your device. I doubt very seriously that they would ever send it back. So that might be a way to raise the gap until you do have coverage everywhere.
